blocks.title: Google Docs Clone
blocks.description: A collaborative document editor demonstrating real-time sync, permissions, and scalable document storage.

blocks.html: End-to-end clone focused on multiplayer editing: multiple users see each other's cursors, edits merge without conflicts (CRDT/OT layer), and documents persist with version history. Suitable as a portfolio piece or starter for team docs products.

blocks.title: High-level system view
blocks.chart:
flowchart TB
  subgraph clients [Clients]
    WebApp[React SPA]
  end
  subgraph gateway [Edge]
    API[API Gateway]
    WS[WebSocket Gateway]
  end
  subgraph services [Services]
    Doc[document-service]
    Auth[auth-service]
    Collab[collaboration-service]
  end
  subgraph data [Data]
    PG[(PostgreSQL)]
    Redis[(Redis presence)]
  end
  WebApp --> API
  WebApp --> WS
  API --> Auth
  API --> Doc
  WS --> Collab
  Doc --> PG
  Collab --> Redis
  Collab --> Doc

items: Clients load document snapshots via REST and subscribe to live ops over WebSockets.
collaboration-service broadcasts operational transforms or CRDT updates to room members.
document-service persists snapshots and append-only revision log for recovery.
auth-service issues JWTs with document-level ACL claims (viewer, commenter, editor).

blocks.title: Feature breakdown
rows: WebSocket rooms per documentId; heartbeat and reconnect
Slate/ProseMirror-style editor with shared cursor overlays
Invite links with role; optional public read-only URL
Named versions + diff preview between revisions
